commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `wacli contacts get-picture` to fetch profile-picture metadata, download the returned JPEG, write it to a file or stdout, and document/test the new contacts command.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: by source inspection, a nil `GetProfilePictureInfo` result from a matching `--existing-id` returns an error in the new command, while current `profile picture-info` treats the same condition as a successful unchanged result. The broader feature is proven by copied live output in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve the PR by fixing the unchanged-result contract, adding coverage, and removing the release-owned changelog edit.

target.title: feat(contacts): add get-picture command to download avatar JPEG
target.body:
## What

Adds `wacli contacts get-picture --jid JID --output PATH` to download a contact's WhatsApp profile picture as a JPEG file (or stream to stdout). Wraps the existing `Client.GetProfilePictureInfo` and then downloads the JPEG bytes from the returned CDN URL.

```bash
wacli contacts get-picture --jid 1234567890@s.whatsapp.net --output /tmp/avatar.jpg
wacli contacts get-picture --jid 1234567890@s.whatsapp.net --output /tmp/avatar.jpg --type image --json
wacli contacts get-picture --jid 1234567890@s.whatsapp.net --output -  # stdout
```

## Why

I'm syncing my WhatsApp contacts into macOS Contacts (one address book for everyone). The macOS WhatsApp app caches a thumbnail at `Media/Profile/<jid>.thumb` for active chats, but **stops caching as soon as the chat is archived** ã even though the avatar still shows up in the UI (loaded in-memory). For archived contacts there is no on-disk thumb to copy into Contacts.app.

`wacli profile picture-info` already returns the CDN URL, but you then have to wrangle `curl` (with the WhatsApp `Referer` quirk in some flows) to actually get the bytes. `contacts get-picture` does the metadata fetch + download + write in one call, with the same flag conventions as the rest of `wacli contacts`.

## How

- `cmd/wacli/contacts_get_picture.go` ã new subcommand registered in `newContactsCmd`. Uses the same `openLiveProfileApp` / `parseProfileTarget` helpers as `profile picture-info`.
- `--type preview` (default) ã 96û96 thumbnail (`preview=true` to whatsmeow)
- `--type image` ã full-size up to 640px (`preview=false`)
- `--output -` ã stream JPEG bytes to stdout
- `--existing-id` ã skip the download when the picture ID matches (mirrors `profile picture-info`)
- `--json` ã metadata (`id`, `type`, `url`, `output`, `bytes`) ã rejected when combined with `--output -` because both would write to stdout
- Maps `whatsmeow.ErrProfilePictureNotSet` ã `no profile picture available` and `whatsmeow.ErrProfilePictureUnauthorized` ã `not authorized to view this profile picture`
- Caps the HTTP response at 5 MiB (full-size WhatsApp pics are ãÊ640px JPEG, well under)
- Writes files with `0600` to match the rest of wacli's owner-only convention
- No new dependencies; uses `net/http` directly. The CDN URL whatsmeow returns is a plain hashed HTTPS URL that does not require auth headers.

## Tests

`cmd/wacli/contacts_get_picture_test.go` ã table-driven unit tests:

- `parsePictureType` ã preview/image/full/invalid
- `wrapProfilePictureError` ã sentinel-error mapping (incl. `errors.Is` wrapping)
- `downloadProfilePicture` ã success, HTTP error, oversize rejection, transport error (via `httpDoer` interface so no real socket is opened)
- `writeProfilePicture` ã file write + perm check, missing parent rejection
- Cobra wiring ã command registered under `contacts`, all four flags exposed
- Flag validation ã `--json + --output -` rejected, `--output` required, bad `--type` rejected
- JSON shape ã required fields present, empty optionals omitted

Local verification on macOS arm64:

```
$ pnpm format:check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build && git diff --check
# all green
$ ./dist/wacli contacts get-picture --jid <active>@s.whatsapp.net --output /tmp/a.jpg
# JPEG 96x96 progressive, 2844 bytes
$ ./dist/wacli contacts get-picture --jid <archived>@s.whatsapp.net --output /tmp/b.jpg
# JPEG 96x96 progressive, 2763 bytes  ã archived case works
$ ./dist/wacli contacts get-picture --jid <active>@s.whatsapp.net --output /tmp/c.jpg --type image --json
{"jid":"...","id":"1022190260","type":"image","url":"https://pps.whatsapp.net/...","output":"/tmp/c.jpg","bytes":48630}
# JPEG 640x640 baseline
```

## Docs

- `CHANGELOG.md` ã entry under `0.11.1 - Unreleased > Added`
- `docs/contacts.md` ã synopsis line + notes paragraph + 2 examples (preview, full-size + json)
target.author: cielecki
